cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Take a morning walk along the creek trail. It's shaded and mellow, great for stretching your legs after the market.
cards.why: Cool temps in the morning make this trail especially pleasant before it warms up.

cards.name: Udon Mugizo
cards.address: 1072 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a bowl of thick, chewy udon. The lunch window is short, so get there right as they open and take your time with it.
cards.why: Udon Mugizo is worth the trip and the lunch hours don't last long.

cards.name: Taking the Stage: 50 Years Ago at the Mayer
cards.blurb: Check out this ongoing exhibition at SCU looking back at the Mayer Theatre's early years. A relaxed afternoon browse through some local performing arts history.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a good way to fill an afternoon without a lot of effort.

cards.name: First Look: Quixote Nuevo
cards.blurb: Head to Santa Clara for the First Look at Quixote Nuevo tonight. It's a free event and a good excuse to see something a little different on a Monday evening.
cards.why: Not every Monday has something worth going out for, but this one does.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head down the Los Gatos Creek Trail for a relaxed walk or bike ride along the water. The shaded path is easy for little legs and genuinely pretty on a cool morning like this.
cards.why: The cooler temps this morning are perfect for a trail walk before it warms up.

cards.name: Art Beat Studio
cards.address: 68 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Art Beat Studio on Campbell Ave and let the kids get hands-on with a creative project. It's a relaxed, drop-in-friendly art space that gives everyone a chance to make something.
cards.why: A low-key creative stop is perfect for burning off some afternoon energy without the chaos.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a picnic lunch at the park, let the kids run around the lake, and make sure you catch the little train. It's a classic for a reason.
cards.why: The train alone will make this the highlight of the trip for younger kids.

cards.name: Game Kastle (Santa Clara)
cards.address: 1350 Coleman Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Game Kastle and let the family dig into board games, card games, or whatever catches the kids' attention on the shelves. The staff are genuinely helpful if you need a recommendation.
cards.why: It's a fun, no-pressure way to wind down the evening with the whole family.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the Los Gatos Creek Trail. It's shaded, flat, and follows the creek the whole way — great for clearing your head on a crisp morning.
cards.why: Cool air, zero crowds on a Monday, and it's a genuinely pretty stretch of trail.

cards.name: Art Beat Studio
cards.address: 68 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Art Beat Studio on Campbell Ave and browse the gallery. It's a relaxed, creative space worth a slow look.
cards.why: A nice low-key afternoon stop after the market while you're already in Campbell.

cards.name: HealthLand Thai Spa
cards.address: 2937 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at HealthLand Thai Spa and get a proper Thai massage. Walk-ins are welcome and the pressure work is legit.
cards.why: Monday is the perfect day to treat yourself before the week actually hits.

cards.name: Social Stitching
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Head to Social Stitching at 110 E Main St in Los Gatos. Bring a project or just show up and see what people are working on. It's a chill, no-pressure hang.
cards.why: It's happening today and it's free — a genuinely fun way to spend an early evening.

cards.name: Jack's Restaurant & Bar
cards.address: 1502 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Jack's Restaurant and Bar in San Jose. Get a cocktail at the bar and order whatever looks good off the menu.
cards.why: Solid food, good drinks, and the kind of place that makes a Monday feel like it was worth it.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the flat creek-side path with the kids. It's easy, shaded, and the water keeps everyone entertained.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect time to get outside before it warms up.

cards.name: Los Gatos Library
cards.address: 100 Villa Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at the Los Gatos Library. Kids section is great, and it's a calm way to end the active part of the day.
cards.why: Free, cozy, and the kids can pick out a book to take home.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona for the afternoon. Walk the lake loop, let the kids run the lawn, and catch a ride on the little Billy Jones Wildcat Railroad if it's running.
cards.why: The lake is beautiful on a partly sunny afternoon and kids genuinely love this park.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 2180 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Yiassoo. Order some Greek plates and share them family-style at the table.
cards.why: Affordable, casual, and easy with kids in tow.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the flat creek-side path with the kids. It's easy, shaded, and the water keeps everyone entertained.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect time to get outside before it warms up.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with something warm and comforting at SaiGon BreadFast. Grab a bánh mì or a Vietnamese breakfast bowl to kick things off right.
cards.why: Cool morning, great food, and it opens right at 9.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Monday Morning Meditation and Mindfulness session in Los Altos. It's a genuinely low-key way to settle into the week.
cards.why: Free, mellow, and a nice reset before the rest of the day.

cards.name: American Cancer Society Discovery Shop - Los Altos, CA
cards.address: 243 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the aisles at the American Cancer Society Discovery Shop on Main Street in Los Altos. You never know what vintage find or hidden gem is waiting.
cards.why: Thrifting in Los Altos hits different, the inventory turns over fast.

cards.name: Urban Plates
cards.address: 300 W McKinley Ave Suite 100, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Urban Plates for a solid lunch. Build your own plate with fresh proteins and seasonal sides, real food done simply.
cards.why: It's a reliable lunch spot that won't slow you down for the afternoon.

cards.name: South Bay Ceramics - Studio 1954
cards.address: 1954 Old Middlefield Way STE I, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk into South Bay Ceramics Studio 1954 in Mountain View and get your hands dirty. They offer open studio sessions where you can throw on the wheel or hand-build.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity and you walk away with something you made.

cards.name: Speculative Fiction BOOK CLUB: Project HAIL MARY
cards.blurb: Wrap the afternoon at the Speculative Fiction Book Club discussing Project Hail Mary. Even if you haven't finished the book, the conversation is worth showing up for.
cards.why: It's happening today and it's free, makes for a great low-effort social stop.
cards.venue: Books Inc Books Inc. Mountain View

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander through the stalls at the Mountain View Farmers Market, let the kids sample fruit, and pick up some snacks for later. It's casual, free, and genuinely fun to poke around.
cards.why: Free, outdoors, and the kids can actually touch and taste things instead of just looking.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head over to Shoreline Park and let the kids run loose along the water. The big open space is perfect for burning off energy, and the views of the Bay are legitimately pretty even on a cloudy day.
cards.why: Kids need room to move and this place delivers in a big way.

cards.name: Speculative Fiction BOOK CLUB: Project HAIL MARY
cards.blurb: Swing by the Speculative Fiction Book Club meetup for Project Hail Mary. Even if you haven't read it, it's a fun crowd and a great excuse to talk sci-fi with people who are really into it.
cards.why: It's happening today and it's free, so why not pop in.
cards.venue: Books Inc Books Inc. Mountain View

cards.name: Leigh's Favorite Books
cards.address: 121 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Leigh's Favorite Books in Sunnyvale. Let each kid pick something out, they probably have a solid kids section and it's a genuinely cozy indie bookshop.
cards.why: A good independent bookstore is always worth an hour, especially with curious kids in tow.

cards.name: California Momo Kitchen
cards.address: 913 E Duane Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at California Momo Kitchen. Momos are basically dumplings and kids almost always go nuts for them. Order a mix of steamed and fried and share everything.
cards.why: Affordable, delicious, and something a little different that the whole family will actually enjoy.

cards.name: Café San José
cards.address: 1583A Meridian Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and breakfast at Café San José, a cozy neighborhood spot perfect for easing into the day.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so get there early and enjoy it while it's fresh and quiet.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library at SJSU and check out the Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection. The work is vivid, political, and genuinely worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free art with real cultural weight, and it's a great way to spend a cool April afternoon indoors.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wander the Campbell Farmers Market and browse the stalls — local produce, flowers, baked goods, and the kind of vendors worth chatting up.
cards.why: Monday morning means smaller crowds and a relaxed pace through the market.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Take a slow walk through the San Jose Municipal Rose Garden. April is when things really start blooming, so the timing is great.
cards.why: One of the best free spots in the South Bay and genuinely beautiful in spring.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and mix and match from the vendors: tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: It's the easiest, most satisfying lunch stop in downtown San Jose.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Restful SPA on Saratoga Ave with a massage or treatment. A solid way to transition out of the afternoon.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly the right move.

cards.name: First Look: Quixote Nuevo
cards.blurb: Catch the First Look reading of Quixote Nuevo at Santa Clara University tonight. It's a free theater event and a genuinely fun way to end the evening.
cards.why: Free theater on a Monday night is a rare and easy yes.

cards.name: Barefoot Coffee
cards.address: 1819 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a coffee and a pastry at Barefoot Coffee. It's cozy, the kids can get a hot chocolate, and it's a low-key way to ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything better, especially before a busy family day.

cards.name: Children's Discovery Museum
cards.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to the Children's Discovery Museum and let the kids go wild. The hands-on exhibits are built for curious minds and there's enough here to keep them genuinely busy for a couple of hours.
cards.why: This is the one stop that was basically designed for exactly this kind of day.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Wander through the Campbell Farmers Market on Campbell Ave. Let the kids pick out something weird they've never tried, grab some fresh fruit for snacking, and just enjoy the whole scene.
cards.why: It's free, it's outdoors, and kids love the energy of a good farmers market.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch along Guadalupe River Park. It's a genuinely pleasant green stretch through downtown, good for letting the kids run a bit and getting some fresh air after being indoors.
cards.why: Free, easy, and a nice way to break up the afternoon without piling everyone into the car.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market. Hit a few different vendors, find a patio table, and let everyone get exactly what they're in the mood for. Tacos for one kid, ramen for another, no arguments.
cards.why: Multiple vendors under one roof means no family standoff over where to eat.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Stop into the Fists of Fury exhibition at King Library. It's a free exhibit on the history of martial arts in Asian American culture, with visuals and artifacts that even kids will find cool.
cards.why: Free, interesting, and a little unexpected for a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: End the day in Japantown. Pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo, grab a bowl of udon at Gombei, and let the kids browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St. It's one of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and genuinely worth an evening stroll.
cards.why: A real neighborhood with great food and something to see around every corner.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a great cup at Kenz Coffee Bar. Grab a seat, order something warm, and ease into the day.
cards.why: A proper coffee stop beats scrambling around on an empty stomach.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto and actually decompress for an hour. It's right on California Ave so the timing works perfectly.
cards.why: A weekday massage is one of those things you never regret doing.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Wander California Ave and browse the stalls. Pick up fresh produce, snacks, or whatever looks good — the market is relaxed and easy to graze through.
cards.why: Monday morning is a mellow time to hit it before the crowds show up.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the galleries at Cantor Arts Center on the Stanford campus. Don't skip the outdoor Rodin sculpture garden, it's one of the best free things in the whole Bay Area.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a cool afternoon is a no-brainer.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head into Kali Greek Kitchen for lunch. Order whatever looks fresh on the menu and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's right there on California Ave and the food is genuinely good.

cards.name: Wildseed
cards.address: South, 855 El Camino Real Building 4, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Wildseed, a plant-forward spot on El Camino with a serious kitchen behind it. The menu is creative and the room feels like a treat without being stuffy.
cards.why: It's a legit dinner spot that actually makes a Monday night feel like something.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Walk the California Ave Farmers Market with the kids, grab some fresh fruit, pastries, or a breakfast bite from the vendors, and let them pick out something fun to snack on.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and it's a genuinely great Monday morning with the market in full swing.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Head over to the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin sculptures outside are a big hit with kids, and the galleries inside are free to explore at whatever pace works for your family.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus is hard to beat on a cool spring morning.

cards.name: Covour Coffee
cards.address: 2706 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Covour Coffee for a proper cup and grab something for the kids too. It's a low-key spot to regroup before heading into the main part of the day.
cards.why: A cozy pit stop that keeps everyone fueled up without breaking the bank.

cards.name: Rinconada park playground
cards.address: 00346006, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Rinconada Park playground and let the kids run loose. It's a great setup with plenty of space to burn off that lunch energy and enjoy the afternoon.
cards.why: The kids will love it and you get some fresh air on a decent afternoon.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Kali Greek Kitchen on California Ave. It's casual, affordable, and the food is fresh and flavorful. Great spot to sit down and recharge before the afternoon.
cards.why: Solid lunch in a relaxed setting that works well for families.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Color Me Mine on University Ave and pick out a piece of pottery to paint. Kids can go wild with the colors and you walk out with something to remember the day by.
cards.why: It's creative, it's indoors, and it's the kind of activity kids ask to do again immediately.
